hero_title: Orchestrate Sanctions and Adverse Media Alerts
hero_title_accent: Compliance Routing for Digital Asset VASPs
hero_subtitle: SFTY connects multi-provider screening outputs to route sanctions and adverse media alerts into structured compliance workflows with audit-ready evidence and case management.
trust_card_1_value: Identity checks connected to onboarding decisions
trust_card_2_value: Corporate verification, UBO review and entity risk
trust_card_3_value: Wallet and transaction monitoring in one workflow
trust_card_4_value: Message routing, completeness checks and exception handling
trust_card_5_value: Regulatory changes mapped to internal rule
trust_card_6_value: Decision logs and evidence for every review
problem_title: Screening alerts slow without clear routing
problem_description: Compliance teams struggle when sanctions and adverse media alerts remain isolated across multiple providers. This creates manual bottlenecks, inconsistent escalation and weak audit evidence for review and reporting in digital asset businesses.
problem_point_1_value: KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule outputs sit in separate tools
problem_point_2_value: Teams move data between dashboards, spreadsheets and internal notes
problem_point_3_value: Regulatory changes are hard to map into internal workflows
problem_point_4_value: Evidence and decision history are difficult to reconstruct later
solution_description: SFTY connects sanctions and adverse media alerts from multiple providers into structured review queues, applies internal policies for escalation, and preserves audit evidence to streamline compliance officer workflows.
workflow_title: From sanctions alerts to case evidence
workflow_description: SFTY routes sanctions and adverse media alerts through review queues, case creation, decision logging and evidence preservation within one structured compliance workflow.
workflow_step_1_description: KYC, KYB, KYT and screening outputs enter SFTY
workflow_step_2_description: Risk rules route checks into the right workflow
workflow_step_3_description: A case opens with customer, wallet and provider context
workflow_step_4_description: Teams review, decide and record the outcome
workflow_step_5_description: Logs, decisions and reports stay ready for audit
why_sfty_title: Beyond fragmented alerts. One routing layer
why_sfty_description: SFTY turns disparate sanctions and adverse media alerts into structured routing, cases, decisions and audit-ready evidence without replacing existing screening providers.
why_sfty_card_1_description: Work above existing KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule tools
why_sfty_card_2_description: Turn disconnected outputs into structured reviews and cases
why_sfty_card_3_description: Preserve evidence, reviewer actions and decision history
why_sfty_card_4_description: Give compliance, risk and leadership one operating view
authority_description: SFTY provides VASP operations with controlled workflows for screening alert escalation, audit-ready evidence preservation, and compliance case governance across enterprise deployments.
authority_card_1_description: Connect KYC, KYB, KYT, Travel Rule and screening providers into one workflow
authority_card_2_description: Preserve provider outputs, reviewer notes, decisions and supporting records
authority_card_3_description: Prepare clearer compliance evidence for banks, partners and external review
authority_card_4_description: Support private environments, permissions, integrations and internal governance
ask_sfty_placeholder: How can SFTY route sanctions and adverse media alerts
ask_sfty_chip_1_message: Explain how screening alerts flow through compliance workflows
ask_sfty_chip_2_message: Show how audit-ready evidence is preserved for screening alerts
ask_sfty_chip_3_message: Describe provider orchestration models for sanctions alert management
ask_sfty_helper_text: AI assistant for product, deployment and compliance questions
faq_description: Clear answers on provider orchestration, Travel Rule workflows, audit evidence and enterprise compliance operations.
faq_1_question: How does SFTY route sanctions alerts?
faq_1_answer: SFTY collects sanctions and adverse media alerts, applies internal policies, and routes them into structured compliance queues and escalation workflows for timely review by operations teams.
faq_2_question: Can SFTY connect multiple screening providers?
faq_2_answer: SFTY sits above screening providers, normalizing and connecting alert outputs from multiple sources into a single operational workflow without requiring provider replacement.
faq_3_question: How does SFTY preserve alert evidence?
faq_3_answer: SFTY preserves case records, decision logs and supporting evidence for sanctions alerts to support audit-ready traceability and defensible compliance reviews.
faq_4_question: How does SFTY integrate with compliance stacks?
faq_4_answer: SFTY connects with existing compliance tools via APIs and configured workflows, enabling controlled deployment and role-based access within current enterprise stacks.
faq_5_question: Does SFTY replace sanctions or screening providers?
faq_5_answer: No. SFTY complements existing sanctions and screening providers by organizing their outputs into structured cases, decisions and reports without replacing them.
final_title: Route sanctions alerts into controlled workflows
final_description: Review how SFTY connects multi-provider screening outputs into structured cases, decision logs and audit-ready compliance evidence.
